title: archangelica villosa
intro: Archangelica villosa, also known as wild angelica, is a perennial herb belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Archangelica villosa include relieving respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as treating digestive problems and menstrual cramps. Archangelica villosa can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is also used in soups, salads, and as a garnish to add flavor and aroma.
